title: I am worthy of millions, simply because I am
subtitle: A present-tense affirmation for claiming the unconditional right to very large wealth.
excerpt: A short affirmation practice from Day 24 of the MONEY program. Use to state the worthiness claim on its own terms, without a list of reasons that have to be earned.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 24 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when the worthiness question is running — when you catch yourself listing reasons you haven't yet qualified for a certain level of wealth.

content.text: An affirmation from Day 24 of the MONEY program. The word "simply" is deliberate: it removes the condition. Worthiness for very large wealth doesn't require a qualifying achievement. It's not earned by working hard enough or being good enough. The affirmation states it as a fact of existence.
Notice whether your instinct is to add a qualifier: "worthy of millions once I've built the business" or "worthy if I work harder". The affirmation strips the qualifier out. That's the work.
I am worthy of millions, simply because I am.
Say it out loud three times in the morning, hand on heart. If you notice resistance to the word "simply", that's the part to stay with.
Adapted from Day 24 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
